visual_elements.description: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness within a system.
In the context of thermodynamics, it's often associated with energy dispersal and distribution across molecules in a substance.
The Second Law of Thermodynamics states that entropy tends to increase over time in an isolated system. This means systems naturally progress from order to disorder.
Real-world examples include the melting of ice into water, where energy disperses and increases the overall entropy of a closed container holding both substances.
